violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1  (Precondition Propagation)
; -------------------------------------------------
; Model of an SMT‑LIB block that has a genuine transition:
;   - two declared constants a, b
;   - one assertion that relates both (b = a - 1)
; According to isVacuous’s algorithm this counts as a transition,
; therefore the function must return false.
; We prove that assuming the function returns true leads to contradiction.

;--- declarations (the “code” that would be inside the block)
(declare-const a Int)
(declare-const b Int)

;--- code‑model transition (the only assertion in the block)
(assert (= b (- a 1)))   ; references both a and b → transitionCount ≥ 1

;--- formalisation of the function’s result:
; 0 = false, 1 = true  (we treat the boolean as an Int for SMT‑LIB)
(declare-const result Int)

; The function would set result = 0 when a transition is found.
; Encode the *negated* property: assume result = 1 (i.e. the function claims
; the block is vacuous) even though we have a transition.
(assert (= result 1))

; Check‑satisfiability:
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat → the assumption that a block with a transition is
; declared vacuous is impossible; thus the property “isVacuous returns false
; whenever a transition exists” is proven.
